title: Salty Silky Quesadilla de Queso
description: A kid-friendly Yucatecan-inspired dish featuring a silky liquid cheese filling, poached for a smooth texture. Suitable for vegan and non-vegan options.

ingredients.name: Plant-based cheese (such as Oaxaca or Queso Fresco)
Corn tortillas
ingredients.notes: Look for vegan and gluten-free options

instructions: In a blender or food processor, combine plant-based cheese, cayenne pepper, and garlic powder. Blend until smooth and creamy.
Heat a small skillet over low heat. Add a tablespoon of olive oil and swirl to coat.
Add the blended cheese mixture to the skillet and cook for 5-7 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the cheese reaches a silky consistency.
Poach the cheese by placing the skillet in a larger pot of boiling water. Cover the pot and cook for 5-7 minutes, or until the cheese has thickened slightly.
Meanwhile, warm tortillas by wrapping them in a damp paper towel and microwaving for 20-30 seconds.
To assemble the quesadillas, place a warmed tortilla on a flat surface. Spoon a quarter of the cheese mixture onto half of the tortilla, leaving a 1-inch border around the edges.
Fold the tortilla in half to enclose the filling. Repeat with the remaining tortillas and cheese mixture.
Heat a large skillet or griddle over medium heat. Cook the quesadillas for 2-3 minutes per side, or until they're crispy and golden brown.
Cut the quesadillas into wedges and serve immediately. Garnish with chopped cilantro and a squeeze of lemon juice, if desired.
